title: Fred Deployment Manifest
description: Schema for Fred deployment manifests. Supports two formats: a single-service DockerManifest or a multi-service StackManifest. For POST /v1/leases/{uuid}/data the raw HTTP body is the manifest JSON. For POST /v1/leases/{uuid}/update the manifest is base64-encoded inside a JSON wrapper ({"payload": "<base64>"}); this schema describes the decoded manifest, not the wrapper.

oneOf.description: Single-service manifest. Non-empty depends_on is forbidden.
Multi-service stack manifest with a top-level services map.

DockerManifestBase.description: Base container specification shared by single-service and stack contexts. Resource limits are determined by the lease SKU, not the manifest.

ports.description: Port mappings. Keys are "port/protocol" strings; values configure optional host port binding.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the container. Certain names and prefixes are blocked for security.

propertyNames.description: Variable name. Cannot be empty, contain '=' or NUL. Blocked: PATH, LD_*, FRED_*, DOCKER_* (case-insensitive).

labels.description: Custom labels added to the container. Keys must not start with "fred." (reserved namespace).

tmpfs.description: Additional writable tmpfs mount paths for containers with read-only root filesystems. /tmp and /run are always mounted automatically.

items.description: Absolute path. Must not be /, /tmp, /run, /proc, /sys, /dev, or any sub-path of /proc, /sys, /dev.

user.description: Container runtime user. Formats: "uid", "uid:gid", "username", "username:group". No whitespace; both user and group parts must be non-empty.

depends_on.description: Startup dependencies on other services. Only allowed inside a StackManifest; rejected for single-service manifests when non-empty.

stop_grace_period.description: Time to wait after SIGTERM before SIGKILL. Must be between 1s and 120s inclusive.

init.description: Run an init process (tini) as PID 1 for zombie reaping and signal forwarding.

expose.description: Inter-service ports documented without creating host bindings. Port numbers as strings, 1-65535, no duplicates.

DockerManifestSingle.description: Single-service manifest. Identical to DockerManifestBase but non-empty depends_on is forbidden (Go: len(m.DependsOn) > 0 is rejected when inStack=false; empty map is allowed).

DockerManifestInStack.description: Service manifest within a stack. depends_on is allowed.

StackManifest.description: Multi-service deployment. Each service has its own DockerManifest. Service names must match chain lease items 1:1.

services.description: Map of service name to DockerManifest. At least one service required.

PortSpec.description: Port specification in "port/protocol" format. Port: 1-65535. Protocol: tcp or udp (case-insensitive, runtime normalizes to lowercase).

host_port.description: Fixed host port. 0 or omitted means Docker assigns a random available port.

ingress.description: Marks this port as the preferred ingress port for FQDN routing. At most one TCP port per manifest may set this. When set, overrides the default selection order (80 > 8080 > lowest).

test.description: Health check command. First element must be CMD, CMD-SHELL, or NONE. CMD and CMD-SHELL require at least one additional argument.

oneOf.description: CMD: exec form, requires command + at least one argument.
CMD-SHELL: shell form, requires the type prefix + shell command string.

timeout.description: Timeout for each health check execution.

retries.description: Number of consecutive failures before marking unhealthy.

start_period.description: Initial grace period during which health check failures are not counted.

DependsOnCondition.description: Condition a dependency must meet before this service starts.

condition.description: service_started: dependency container has started. service_healthy: dependency must have an active health check and be healthy.

Duration.description: A time duration. Accepts Go duration strings ("30s", "1m") or integer nanoseconds.

StopGracePeriod.description: Duration bounded to 1s–120s. Integer nanoseconds are range-checked exactly. For strings, integer seconds (1s–120s), milliseconds (1000ms–120000ms), and minutes (1m–2m) are enforced by the schema. Fractional and compound forms (e.g., "1.5s", "1m30s") pass the schema and are range-validated at runtime.

oneOf.description: Fractional single-unit duration. Range validated at runtime.
Compound duration (e.g., "1m30s"). Range validated at runtime.

ServiceName.description: RFC 1123 DNS label: lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens, 1-63 characters, must start and end with alphanumeric.
